name: Surya OCR
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-3.0); free for non-commercial; commercial license available

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub for model download

performance.notes: GPU recommended; ~1s/page GPU, ~5s/page CPU; faster than Tesseract for complex layouts

agent_readiness.notes: State-of-the-art open-source OCR by the marker-pdf author. Multi-lingual OCR (90+ languages). Text detection, recognition, line/word segmentation. Layout detection. Table recognition. Reading order detection. Significantly better than Tesseract for modern documents and complex layouts. Used under the hood by marker-pdf for PDF-to-Markdown conversion. Python API. Requires GPU for practical throughput.

gotchas: GPL-3.0 license (commercial use requires paid license)
GPU required for practical throughput (CPU very slow)
Newer project — less battle-tested than Tesseract
Some languages perform worse than English
best_when: Modern document OCR surpassing Tesseract quality
Document AI pipelines needing layout-aware OCR
PDF/image to structured text for RAG pipelines
summary: Surya is a state-of-the-art open-source OCR library supporting 90+ languages by the marker-pdf author. Significantly better than Tesseract for modern documents and complex layouts. Handles text detection, layout analysis, table recognition, and reading order. Used by marker-pdf for PDF conversion. GPL-3.0 license — commercial use requires paid license. GPU required for practical throughput.
